title: apium ternatum var. genuinum
intro: Apium ternatum var. genuinum, also known as wild celery, is a type of wild plant that belongs to the parsley family (Apiaceae) and is native to North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as a diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing blood pressure, alleviating symptoms of arthritis and gout, and supporting digestive health. Wild celery can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
